title: Tautological relation guard makes insert_rejected branch dead code

findingDetails.evidence: enabledRelation() returns RELATION_TYPES.ENABLED only if Object.values(RELATION_TYPES) includes it; ENABLED is always a member, so it never returns null and the `if (!relation)` -> 'insert_rejected' branch at line 197-200 can never execute.
findingDetails.why: A guard that reads as a validation gate but can never trip is a maintenance trap; a future reader may assume the relation is validated against runtime data when it is a compile-time constant.
findingDetails.recommendation: Drop the tautological check and return RELATION_TYPES.ENABLED directly, or replace it with a real validation against the live schema/enabled-relation set if dynamic validation was the intent.
